harnesses.name: Aider
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
read:
  - CONVENTIONS.md
model: anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514
auto-commits: false
test-cmd: pnpm test
harnesses.notes:
Aider has no skill or MCP system. CONVENTIONS.md is read-only context, not a config target.
.aider.conf.yml is the actual config; we generate both.

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
# Project Instructions

## Stack
Next.js 14 + Prisma + Postgres.

## Conventions
- TypeScript strict mode
- Functional components with hooks
- Test files colocated as `*.test.ts`

## Tools
See `~/.claude/skills/` for installed skills and `.mcp.json` for MCP servers.
harnesses.notes:
Skills live in ~/.claude/skills/ (global) or .claude/skills/ (project).
MCP servers configured via .mcp.json or ~/.claude/settings.json.
Reads CLAUDE.md hierarchically from cwd up to home dir.

harnesses.name: Cline
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
---
description: "Project rules"
paths: ["src/**"]
---
# Rules

Use TypeScript strict mode. Prefer functional patterns.
Never commit `.env` or secrets.
harnesses.notes:
.clinerules can be a single file or a directory of files. Each file may have YAML frontmatter.
Strong MCP support; reads .clineignore for files to skip.

harnesses.name: Codex CLI
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
# Project

## Setup
`pnpm install && pnpm dev`

## Testing
`pnpm test` runs Vitest. Integration tests under `test/integration/`.

## Code style
TypeScript strict. ESLint config in repo root.
harnesses.notes: AGENTS.md is the canonical config file. Codex was the original driver of the AGENTS.md open standard.

harnesses.name: Continue
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
name: my-project
version: 0.0.1
schema: v1
rules:
  - "Use TypeScript for all new files"
  - "Keep functions under 50 lines"
models:
  - name: Claude Opus
    provider: anthropic
    model: claude-opus-4-20250514
harnesses.notes:
Continue migrated from config.json to config.yaml in 2025. Both still supported.
Custom commands defined inline in config; MCP via mcpServers block.

harnesses.name: Cursor
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
---
description: "React component conventions"
globs: ["src/components/**/*.tsx"]
alwaysApply: false
---
Use functional components with hooks. No class components.
Default exports for components, named exports for utilities.
harnesses.notes:
.cursorrules is legacy but still read. New projects should use .cursor/rules/*.mdc.
Supports MCP via Cursor Settings > MCP. AGENTS.md is read as a fallback.

harnesses.name: Gemini CLI
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
# Project: TypeScript Library

## Coding Style
- Use 2 spaces, strict mode
- Interface names prefixed with `I`

## References
@./style-guide.md
harnesses.notes:
GEMINI.md supports `@./path` imports to pull in additional context files.
MCP servers configured via ~/.gemini/settings.json.

harnesses.name: OpenHands
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
---
name: repo-conventions
agent: CodeActAgent
---
This repo uses pnpm workspaces. Always run `pnpm install` from root.
Tests use Vitest. Lint with ESLint + Prettier.
harnesses.notes:
OpenHands uses "microagents" — markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Repo-wide ones live
in .openhands/microagents/repo.md; keyword-triggered ones in keyword.md.
MCP support is current; was previously planned-only.

harnesses.name: Windsurf
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
Project: Full-stack React + Node
Code style: ESLint config in repo root
Database: Postgres via Prisma — never write raw SQL outside migrations
Tests: Vitest, colocated as `*.test.ts`
harnesses.notes:
.windsurfrules is plaintext. Newer .windsurf/rules/ directory supports Markdown with frontmatter.
Reads AGENTS.md as a fallback.

harnesses.name: Zed
harnesses.exampleSnippet:
Project conventions:
- TypeScript strict mode
- Tests required for new modules
- Use pnpm, never npm
harnesses.notes:
Zed reads many file formats in priority order: .rules, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules,
.clinerules, AGENT.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md.
We default to .rules; AGENTS.md is also picked up.

stacks.name: FastAPI + Postgres
stacks.description: Python FastAPI backend with SQLAlchemy and Postgres. Suits API-first services and AI/ML backends.
conventions: Python 3.11+ with type hints on every function signature.
Pydantic v2 for request/response schemas.
Alembic for database migrations; never alter schema by hand.
Ruff for lint+format, mypy for type checking.
Tests with pytest, fixtures in conftest.py, factory-boy for test data.

stacks.name: Mobile (Expo + React Native)
stacks.description: Cross-platform mobile app with Expo and React Native. Suits prototypes and small teams shipping to iOS+Android from one codebase.
conventions: Expo SDK (managed workflow) unless a feature truly requires bare workflow.
expo-router for file-based navigation.
TypeScript strict; React Native Paper or NativeWind for UI.
EAS Build for distribution, EAS Update for OTA updates.
Test on physical devices before merging; simulator is not enough.

stacks.name: Next.js + Prisma + Postgres
stacks.description: Next.js App Router with Prisma ORM and Postgres. Suits production web apps that need auth, DB, and SSR.
conventions: Next.js App Router only — no pages/ directory in new code.
Server Components by default; mark Client Components explicitly with 'use client'.
Database access goes through Prisma; never raw SQL outside migrations.
Environment variables typed via @t3-oss/env-nextjs or similar.
Tailwind for styling; component library is shadcn/ui.

stacks.name: React + Node
stacks.description: Full-stack JavaScript with React frontend and Node.js backend (Express/Fastify). Default for SaaS prototypes and internal tools.
conventions: Use TypeScript strict mode across both frontend and backend.
Prefer functional React components with hooks; no class components.
API routes return typed responses; share types from a /shared package.
Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for end-to-end.
ESLint + Prettier, formatted on save.

stacks.name: Rust CLI
stacks.description: Rust command-line tool with clap and serde. Suits distributable binaries and systems tools.
conventions: Rust 2021 edition or later, MSRV declared in Cargo.toml.
clap for CLI parsing, anyhow for application errors, thiserror for library errors.
Tests with cargo test; integration tests under tests/.
Clippy clean — `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`.
Release binaries via cargo-dist.
